heading: Low-confidence replies
text: When the system cannot determine the customer's product interest or buying stage, it should ask one concise clarifying question instead of guessing.

heading: Sensitive commercial requests
text: Requests involving custom discounts, refunds, legal concerns or unusual delivery commitments must be routed to a human reviewer before any customer-facing answer is sent.

heading: Campaign attribution
text: Campaign/source context should confirm likely intent, but it should not override explicit customer messages or approved response rules.

heading: Human handoff
text: A handoff is required when the lead asks for a non-standard condition, when the retrieved context conflicts, or when the system cannot find an approved source.

heading: Product specificity
text: When the product category is known, replies should mention only that category and avoid introducing unrelated products.
